# Release-please configuration

This directory contains [release-please](https://github.com/googleapis/release-please)
configuration that automates version bumps, CHANGELOG entries, and
release tags for every crate in the repository.

## Files

- `release-please-config.json` — per-crate release configuration
  (`release-type`, `package-name`, tag `component`, changelog path).
- `.release-please-manifest.json` — current "released" version of every
  package. release-please updates this in the same PR that bumps the
  Cargo.toml versions.
- `workflows/release-please.yml` — the workflow that runs release-please
  on every push to `main` and opens / maintains a release PR per crate.

## How it works

1. Contributors land commits on `main` using
   [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) with a
   crate scope, e.g.:
   ```
   feat(internal): add foo/bar endpoint
   fix(firmware): pin embassy-usb-driver to 0.2.0
   feat(application)!: rename --baud to --baud-rate
   ```
2. release-please reads the commit log since the last release tag for
   each crate and opens a release PR per crate (`separate-pull-requests:
   true`), e.g. `chore(internal): release 0.6.0`.
3. The release PR includes:
   - bumped `Cargo.toml` version (all seven released crates move
     together — see "Linked versions" below)
   - bumped path-dependency `version = "..."` entries in dependent
     crates' Cargo.tomls — auto-rewritten by the `cargo-workspace`
     plugin for host-workspace members; the firmware crate's
     `pico-de-gallo-internal` dep spec still requires a one-line
     manual edit (see "Firmware dep-spec edit (manual)" below)
   - regenerated `Cargo.lock` files (auto-committed by the lockfile
     guard CI job, or do this locally before merging)
   - CHANGELOG entry generated from the conventional-commit log
   - manifest update
4. Merging the release PR creates the GitHub Release **and** the tag
   (e.g. `internal-v0.6.0`). The existing
   `release-{firmware,ffi,application,pyco}.yml` workflows still fire
   on those tags and produce binary artifacts.

## Tag conventions

| Crate                    | Component  | Tag prefix       |
|--------------------------|------------|------------------|
| `pico-de-gallo-internal` | `internal` | `internal-v*`    |
| `pico-de-gallo-lib`      | `library`  | `library-v*`     |
| `pico-de-gallo-hal`      | `hal`      | `hal-v*`         |
| `pico-de-gallo-ffi`      | `ffi`      | `ffi-v*`         |
| `gallo` (CLI)            | `application` | `application-v*` |
| `pyco-de-gallo`          | `pyco`     | `pyco-v*`        |
| `pico-de-gallo-firmware` | `firmware` | `firmware-v*`    |

`hardware-v*` (KiCad gerbers) is **not** managed by release-please —
hardware revisions move on a different cadence. Cut hardware tags
manually as before.

## Wire-protocol invariant

`pico-de-gallo-internal`'s `[package].version` is reflected at runtime
via `SCHEMA_VERSION_{MAJOR,MINOR,PATCH}` (auto-generated by
`build.rs`). When wire types change:

- bump **`pico-de-gallo-internal`** with `feat!` or `BREAKING CHANGE:`
  (pre-1.0: minor bump)
- the `linked-versions` plugin (see below) bumps **every other
  released component** in the same release cycle, so the firmware
  encodes the new schema version and every host surface sees the
  new types

The `linked-versions` plugin enforces lockstep version *numbers*,
but it cannot tell that the firmware is wire-coupled to internal at
the bytes-on-the-wire level — the firmware source still needs the
matching schema-encoding change. That discipline is on the
contributor and reviewer.

## Linked versions

All seven released components — `internal`, `library`, `hal`, `ffi`,
`application`, `pyco`, `firmware` — are grouped under a single
`linked-versions` plugin entry in `release-please-config.json`. A
Conventional Commit that bumps any one of them causes release-please
to open release PRs that move **all seven** to the same new
version. This trades release-noise for a guarantee that wire-coupled
components never drift in version space.

Trade-off: every release-please PR cycle ships seven crate releases
even when only one crate has source changes. The CHANGELOG of the
unchanged crates will note the version bump but no notable changes.
That's the price for never re-living the §13.17 (AGENTS.md, row
2026-06-11) divergence regression.

### How the two plugins interact

Two release-please plugins are configured together, and the order
and `"merge"` flags matter:

1. **`cargo-workspace`** (`"merge": false`) — walks the host
   workspace at the repository root, finds every member whose
   `[package].version` is being bumped by the linked-versions
   policy, and rewrites all other members' `version = "..."` dep
   specs to match. Without this, bumping `pico-de-gallo-internal`
   from 0.5.0 to 0.6.0 in its own `Cargo.toml` would leave
   `pico-de-gallo-lib`'s `pico-de-gallo-internal = { version =
   "0.5.0", path = "..." }` spec stale, breaking `cargo build`
   on the next commit and shipping a broken `lib 0.6.0` to
   crates.io that depends on `internal ^0.5.0`.

2. **`linked-versions`** — groups all seven released components
   (six host + firmware) so they share one combined release PR
   with matching version numbers. This is what gives us the
   wire-protocol lockstep guarantee.

The `"merge": false` on `cargo-workspace` is essential: both
plugins want to merge candidate PRs into one combined PR, and
upstream release-please issue #1457 documents that they collide
unless `cargo-workspace` yields. With `false`, the
`linked-versions` plugin owns the final candidate merge and
`cargo-workspace` runs purely as a dep-spec rewriter on top.

### Firmware dep-spec edit (manual)

The `cargo-workspace` plugin walks only the host workspace's
members. Firmware (`crates/pico-de-gallo-firmware`) is excluded
from the host workspace (it targets `thumbv8m.main-none-eabihf`
and is no_std), so its `pico-de-gallo-internal = { version = "X",
path = "../pico-de-gallo-internal" }` dep spec is NOT auto-bumped.

When a combined release PR lands a new `internal` minor:

1. Edit `crates/pico-de-gallo-firmware/Cargo.toml` so the
   `pico-de-gallo-internal` dep spec's `version = "..."` matches
   the new internal version (e.g. `0.5.0` → `0.6.0`).
2. Refresh `crates/pico-de-gallo-firmware/Cargo.lock`:
   ```bash
   cd crates/pico-de-gallo-firmware
   cargo update -p pico-de-gallo-internal --locked
   ```
3. Stage both files into the release-please PR before merging.

This is documented in AGENTS.md §6.5 as part of the wire-bump
checklist. The release-please PR's CI will fail with a `cargo
check --locked` error if you forget either step.

## Manual version bumps are forbidden

**Never edit `[package].version` in any `Cargo.toml` by hand.** Doing
so desyncs the in-repo version from the manifest
(`.github/.release-please-manifest.json`) and from crates.io.
release-please then reads the manifest, sees `Cargo.toml ==
manifest + N`, concludes there are no pending releases, and opens
**no** release PR — even though your fix is sitting on `main`,
unreleased. Recovery requires a single coordinated re-release PR
that bumps source *and* manifest to a new target version, then
manual tagging post-merge. See AGENTS.md §13.17 (row 2026-06-11)
for the playbook.

## Bootstrap

`bootstrap-sha` in `release-please-config.json` is set to the commit
that finalized the 0.9.0 release. It is **only consulted when the
manifest is missing an entry for a package** — release-please then
walks back to bootstrap-sha to count Conventional Commits. Once the
manifest has an entry for a package (as ours does for all seven),
the manifest version is the source of truth and bootstrap-sha is
effectively dormant.

## Known limitations

- Existing pre-bootstrap commits are **not** in Conventional Commits
  format, so the very first release PR will be empty until at least one
  conventional commit lands on `main`.
- The repo has **two separate Cargo workspaces** (host workspace
  manifest at the repository root, firmware workspace at
  `crates/pico-de-gallo-firmware/`). Each workspace's `Cargo.lock`
  must be regenerated when path-dep versions change. CI's
  `lockfile` job catches forgotten regenerations.
- The `cargo-workspace` plugin rewrites cross-crate `version = "..."`
  dep specs only for members of the host workspace at the repo root.
  Firmware (`crates/pico-de-gallo-firmware`) is a separate workspace
  and is **not** walked by the plugin; its `pico-de-gallo-internal`
  dep spec is hand-edited per release (see "Firmware dep-spec edit
  (manual)" above). Semver-incompatible breaks across crates still
  need contributor judgment about whether a major bump is warranted.
